description:
This figure presents two phase-time plots, (a) and (b), which illustrate concepts related to phase unwrapping in Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) data analysis. Both plots have phase (φ) on the y-axis and time on the x-axis.

Chart (a) displays two sets of data points: light blue triangles, which show a decreasing trend with a periodic wrap-around behaviour, and black triangles, which represent a segment of phase data also showing a decreasing trend. A red dotted line indicates a linear regression fit to the black triangle data. A vertical double-headed arrow annotated "2π" shows the difference between the lower bound of the black triangle data and the extrapolated red dotted line, suggesting a phase ambiguity or the need for a 2π shift.

Chart (b) also displays light blue triangles showing a decreasing, wrapped phase trend. It includes black triangles, representing a different segment of phase data, and red triangles, which appear to be an unwrapped or shifted version of the black triangles. A red dotted line represents a linear regression fit to the red triangle data. Similar to chart (a), a vertical double-headed arrow annotated "2π" indicates the vertical difference between the lowest point of the black triangle data and the corresponding point on the red dotted line representing the red triangle trend, further highlighting the 2π phase ambiguity.
